text.title: gitlab-cicd Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for gitlab-cicd. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Use rules: instead of only/except for all conditional job execution.
rule.rationale: only/except is deprecated and cannot combine conditions. rules: supports if/changes/exists combinators and per-rule variable overrides.

rule.statement: Use needs: to form a DAG — jobs start when their specific dependencies finish, not after the whole stage finishes.
rule.rationale: Stage-based execution forces serial stage gates. needs: allows test and lint to run in parallel after build, cutting wall-clock time by 30-60%.

rule.statement: Keep compiled outputs in artifacts, keep dependency folders in cache.
rule.rationale: Artifacts are guaranteed delivery between jobs. Cache is best-effort and shared across pipelines. Mixing them causes missing dist/ in deploy jobs.

rule.statement: Scope cache keys with a lockfile hash to invalidate when dependencies change.
rule.rationale: A stale cache with the wrong package versions causes intermittent test failures that are hard to reproduce.

rule.statement: Parallelize independent jobs using needs: [] or needs: [specific-job] rather than stage ordering.
rule.rationale: With stage ordering, lint waits for all build jobs. With needs: lint can run in parallel with other test jobs as soon as its single dependency finishes.

rule.statement: Set expire_in on all artifacts. Use 1 day for CI intermediates, longer only for release artifacts.
rule.rationale: Without expiry, artifacts accumulate indefinitely. GitLab default is 30 days which fills storage.

rule.statement: Include GitLab security templates via include: rather than writing custom SAST/DAST jobs.
rule.rationale: GitLab-maintained templates stay updated with new vulnerability signatures. Custom jobs require ongoing maintenance.

rule.statement: Mark all CI/CD secrets as Protected AND Masked in GitLab variable settings.
rule.rationale: Protected limits the variable to protected branches/tags. Masked prevents the value from appearing in job logs. Both are required; neither alone is sufficient.

rule.statement: Define environment: name and url on every deploy job, and pair with an on_stop job.
rule.rationale: Environments enable deployment tracking, approval workflows, and rollback. Without on_stop, review namespaces accumulate and consume cluster resources.

rule.statement: Enable Docker BuildKit and use --cache-from with the latest tag to speed up image builds.
rule.rationale: Without layer cache reuse, every build starts from scratch. BuildKit parallelizes layer builds and enables cache import/export.

rule.statement: Use when: manual for production deployments; use automated sync for staging.
rule.rationale: Production requires human gate. Staging should be continuously deployed to catch integration issues early.
